name: RadiotherapyPhasePrescription
title: Radiotherapy Phase Prescription
description:
A prescription for a radiotherapy treatment phase.
In ARIA, a prescription can cover a single plan or complete phase implemented with one or multiple plans. During prescription it may not even be defined whether
multiple plans will be used. Therefore, all ARIA prescriptions are exposed as Phase Prescriptions.

element.short: A request for a service to be performed

extension.valueMarkdown: When a resource has no narrative, only systems that fully understand the data can display the resource to a human safely. Including a human readable representation in the resource makes for a much more robust eco-system and cheaper handling of resources by intermediary systems. Some ecosystems restrict distribution of resources to only those systems that do fully understand the resources, and as a consequence implementers may believe that the narrative is superfluous. However experience shows that such eco-systems often open up to new participants over time.

The reference may be version specific or not. If the reference is not to a FHIR RESTful server, then it should be assumed to be version specific. References are URLs that are relative to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/ e.g. "Patient" is a reference to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Patient. element.definition: An identifier for the target resource. This is used when there is no way to reference the other resource directly, either because the entity it represents is not available through a FHIR server, or because there is no way for the author of the resource to convert a known identifier to an actual location. There is no requirement that a Reference.identifier point to something that is actually exposed as a FHIR instance, but it SHALL point to a business concept that would be expected to be exposed as a FHIR instance, and that instance would need to be of a FHIR resource type allowed by the reference.
element.comment:
When an identifier is provided in place of a reference, any system processing the reference will only be able to resolve the identifier to a reference if it understands the business context in which the identifier is used. Sometimes this is global (e.g. a national identifier) but often it is not. For this reason, none of the useful mechanisms described for working with references (e.g. chaining, includes) are possible, nor should servers be expected to be able resolve the reference. Servers may accept an identifier based reference untouched, resolve it, and/or reject it - see CapabilityStatement.rest.resource.referencePolicy. 

When both an identifier and a literal reference are provided, the literal reference is preferred. Applications processing the resource are allowed - but not required - to check that the identifier matches the literal reference

Applications converting a logical reference to a literal reference may choose to leave the logical reference present, or remove it.

Reference is intended to point to a structure that can potentially be expressed as a FHIR resource, though there is no need for it to exist as an actual FHIR resource instance - except in as much as an application wishes to actual find the target of the reference. The content referred to be the identifier must meet the logical constraints implied by any limitations on what resource types are permitted for the reference.  For example, it would not be legitimate to send the identifier for a drug prescription if the type were Reference(Observation|DiagnosticReport).  One of the use-cases for Reference.identifier is the situation where no FHIR representation exists (where the type is Reference (Any).

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Only the following status values are supported for Radiotherapy Prescribed Phases from ARIA: draft | active | revoked | completed | entered-in-error
constraint.expression: status = 'draft' or status = 'active' or status = 'revoked' or status = 'completed' or status = 'entered-in-error'

element.short:
Resource id assigned by ARIA. This id is used to reference this resource from other resources.
  Do not use this id or parts of it for other purposes. See element 'identifier' instead.
Revision number that is updated with each modification.
Date and time of the last modification.
Profiles that this resource complies to.
Gating method selected in ARIA prescription.
Modalities and techniques defined in the prescription.
Treatment modality mapped to mCODE compliant modality code where possible.
Treatment technique mapped to mCODE compliant modality code where possible
Dose prescribed to each volume in the prescription.
Volume to which dose is prescribed in ARIA.
For prescription, ARIA does not create BodyStructures.
For prescription, ARIA exposes the identifier (display name) of the target volume defined in the prescription.
Display name for the target volume in the ARIA prescription.
Dose per fraction in [cGy] prescribed to this volume.
Total dose in [cGy] prescribed to this volume.

Therapeutic intent. Mapped from the intent assigned to the course in ARIA. A value from ARIA's configurable ValueSet http://varian.com/fhir/CodeSystem/radiotherapy-therapeutic-intent is exposed and - if possible - mapped to an additional mCODE compliant code.

constraint.human: Only Phase Ids 'Primary' or 'Boost' are supported. This is a current ARIA limitation. The Phase Id is modelled as Identifier to support more general identifiers in the future.

element.short: Display name is the Prescription ID in ARIA and unique for the patient.
ARIA identifier that is unique for this instance of ARIA and serves as primary database key.
Identifier that annotates which treatement phase is covered by this prescription. ARIA supports 'Primary' or 'Boost'.
Prescriptions from ARIA reference a ServiceRequest that complies to RadiotherapyCoursePrescription.
Name of the Course in case of ARIA prescriptions.
ARIA references the Radiotherapy Course Prescription (parent of all prescriptions belonging to the same Course).

element.definition:
* Status Mapping:
  * (ARIA) reviewed  → (FHIR) draft
  * (ARIA) draft  → (FHIR) draft
  * (ARIA) approved  → (FHIR) active
  * (ARIA) errored  → (FHIR) entered-in-error
  * (ARIA) retired  → (FHIR) revoked.

element.short: Fixed code 'original-order' as defined by CodeX RT.
Fixed code for radiotherapy defined by CodeX RT.
Fixed code for radiotherapy treatment phase defined by CodeX RT.
Patient who receives or received the radiotherapy treatment described in this resource.
Name of the patient 'Last Name, First Name'.
Prescribed treatment frequency. Supported values are exposed as ValueSet http://varian.com/fhir/ValueSet/radiotherapy-prescription-frequency.

element.definition: The date and time of approval.
User who approved the prescription.

element.short: Codes of the assigned diagoses. Note that in ARIA, multiple alternative codes may be assigned to each single diagnosis.
References to the Condition resources that represent the assigned diagnoses. May reference multiple Condition resources for the same diagnosis if alternative code systems are used.
Body site to which radiotherapy is prescribed.
element.comment:
The body site of an ARIA Prescription is bound to a user editable list
and in FHIR exposed as CodeSystem http://varian.com/fhir/CodeSystem/radiotherapy-prescription-site.
Additionally, the code is mapped to SNOMED CT codes of the mCODE Radiotnerapy Treatment Location ValueSet where possible.

element.definition: Free text note.
